description:
You are acting as an observability engineer who is about to tweak and test some
    dashboard-rendering code.  
    For a clean experiment you must prepare an **isolated Python package
    environment** and then produce a short, machine-readable report that lists the
    exact package versions now present in that environment.

    Objectives (all are mandatory):

    1. Create a Python virtual environment named “obs_env” directly under
       /home/user (full path: **/home/user/obs_env**).
       • Any tool is acceptable (`python -m venv`, `virtualenv`, etc.),
         but the resulting directory hierarchy must reside at exactly that path.

    2. Inside that virtual environment install **exactly** the following two
       packages at the specified versions (no additional packages except what
       the installer itself must pull in as dependencies):
       • prometheus-client==0.17.1  
       • grafanalib==0.6.0

    3. Still inside the activated virtual environment, create a plain-text file
       named **package_report.log** in the top-level of the virtual environment
       directory (full path: /home/user/obs_env/package_report.log).

       The file must contain *only* two non-empty lines, one per package, in
       strict ASCII, sorted alphabetically by package name, and following this
       exact format:

           grafanalib==0.6.0
           prometheus-client==0.17.1

       • No blank lines before, after, or between.  
       • End the last line with a single UNIX newline (`\n`).  
       • Do not include any other text or comment lines.

    4. Deactivate the virtual environment when finished (this simply ensures the
       global shell prompt is not left inside the venv; there is no deliverable
       file for this point).

    Validation notes (what automated tests will check):

    • Directory /home/user/obs_env/ exists and looks like a Python virtualenv
      (e.g. contains “bin/activate”).  
    • The two required packages are installed *inside* that environment at the
      exact versions specified (verified via `pip show` inside the venv).  
    • File /home/user/obs_env/package_report.log exists and its contents are
      byte-for-byte identical to the two-line snippet shown above, including the
      trailing newline on the second line.

    Hint: achieving the above normally requires no more than 2–3 shell
    commands—that is the intended complexity scope.
truth:
Expected artefacts after successful completion:

    1. Directory: /home/user/obs_env/  
       • Must contain subdirectories bin/, lib/, include/ as produced by
         python-venv tooling.  
       • Activation script path that tests will use:
         /home/user/obs_env/bin/activate

    2. Installed packages inside that virtual environment (output of
       `pip freeze`, order not important for this internal check):
         grafanalib==0.6.0
         prometheus-client==0.17.1

    3. File: /home/user/obs_env/package_report.log  
       Exact file content (11 bytes + newline each line, total 34 bytes):

           grafanalib==0.6.0\n
           prometheus-client==0.17.1\n

       (`\n` denotes the single 0x0A newline character.)

    Automated verifier steps (not shown to agent):
       a. [ -d /home/user/obs_env ]  
       b. source /home/user/obs_env/bin/activate && \
          pip show grafanalib | grep -Fx "Version: 0.6.0"  
       c. same for prometheus-client 0.17.1  
       d. cmp --silent /home/user/obs_env/package_report.log \
          <(printf 'grafanalib==0.6.0\nprometheus-client==0.17.1\n')

    No other files, directories or package versions are required.
